title: Choice Begins
challenge: With your own free will, choose one food or drink today without guilt, pressure, or a sense of should.
prompt: What did it feel like to make a choice that belonged to you, even if it was small?
celebrationPrompt: What are you celebrating about your ability to choose today?
quote: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
author: Bible

title: Naming the Pressure
challenge: Write down one belief, rule, or expectation that feels imposed and why it no longer serves you.
prompt: Which outside voice has shaped you the most, and what do you want to believe instead?
celebrationPrompt: What pressure did you name today that felt important to see clearly?
quote: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.

title: Pause Before Autopilot
challenge: Notice one automatic habit today and pause for one breath before choosing your response.
prompt: Where did you catch yourself on autopilot, and what changed when you paused?
celebrationPrompt: What is one small pause you are proud of today?
quote: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
author: Bible

title: I Choose
challenge: Practice saying “I choose” at least once today when you describe a decision or next step.
prompt: How does your body respond when you say “I choose” instead of “I have to”?
celebrationPrompt: What choice felt more powerful when you named it out loud?

title: What I Want Instead
challenge: Reflect on one place where expectations have boxed you in, then write what you want instead.

title: Scarcity to Generosity
